name: Architext 1.7.8 Doctor Recovery + Fail-Loud Repairs
summary: Doctor's release-truth repair becomes trustworthy end to end. Enumeration is a union of index-named and dir-discovered detail files shared by status and apply (release_truth_repair_plan), so a detail file on disk but omitted from the index — the roboticus field incident — now yields the previously unreachable 'add <id> to Release Truth history' repair, and advertised repairs always match applied ones. Imperfect indexed details are recovered rather than gated, dropped, or written with null fields: originals are backed up under timestamped .bak names (never clobbered, all-or-nothing — any backup failure aborts every write), missing fields are backfilled from the index summary then explicit defaults, and each recovery is recorded in docs/architext/data/repair-advice.json, whose standing instruction makes reconciling backup vs recovered content the maintaining agent's responsibility (surfaced via status/doctor output and the synced instruction contract). Repairs are fail-loud: every repair category reports write failures per change (DoctorRepair gains additive status/error), doctor and sync print FAILED entries and exit nonzero, and a failed write is never reported as applied. The recovery machinery lives in the new domain::release_recovery module.
updateSource: All work merged to develop via swarm-reviewed PRs #259, #260, #261 (ten checkpoints, each QA+Audit reviewed with adversarial fixtures before advancing; one maintainer escalation E-1 resolved by directive: backup + best-effort unmarshal + normalized re-marshal + reconciliation advice). Core 157/157, CLI 38/38, serve suites green, clippy clean. E2E: 30-release fixture with planted stray JSON, data-root index, suffix-colliding detail names, read-only files — recovery, attribution, and abort semantics all verified against the built binary. Release pending: GitHub release v1.7.8 with binaries + SHA256SUMS not yet published.

required.title: Status and apply share one release-truth repair plan; omitted on-disk details surface
required.summary: Field-confirmed defect (roboticus): a detail file on disk but missing from an existing index was invisible — both status and apply enumerated detail files FROM the index, making the 'add <id> to Release Truth history' change unreachable while roadmap targetReleaseId refs failed validation. Enumeration is now a union (index-named files trusted, dir-discovered files through a strict shape filter requiring all seven summary-source fields non-blank), deduped by components-normalized path AND by id (validation has no duplicate-id check, so duplicates would persist as silent corruption). One shared release_truth_repair_plan feeds both sides, so advertised and applied repairs cannot diverge; a committed status/apply agreement test pins it.

required.title: Backup + best-effort recovery + reconciliation advice for imperfect release details
required.summary: Maintainer directive E-1: incomplete or unparseable index-named details are recovered, not gated/dropped/null-written. Every file the repair overwrites is first backed up with a timestamped .bak name (names never clobber — -N suffix; backups-first all-or-nothing — any backup failure aborts every write loudly). Recovery keeps original fields, backfills from the index summary then explicit defaults (placeholder targetWindow for draft/planned/implementing; a fabricated releasedAt would be invented history, so completed-without-releasedAt stays a validation flag), and re-marshals schema-valid. Each recovery appends to docs/architext/data/repair-advice.json — file, backup path, backfilled fields, and a standing instruction that reconciling old vs new is the maintaining agent's responsibility; entries supersede per file, a corrupt ledger is backed up before reset, status/doctor print pending-advice counts, and the synced instruction section plus canonical AGENTS_APPENDIX document the contract (with a parity test pinning the Rust const to the markdown).

required.title: Repairs report write failures; a failed write is never claimed as applied
required.summary: Every repair category fn returns per-change outcomes carrying the real write error (RepairOutcome); DoctorRepair serializes an additive status: applied|failed (+error) for CLI and serve consumers. Failures attach to exactly the change they defeat — matched on the full file key after review caught a suffix-collision mis-attribution that could mark successful writes as failed. backup_file reports the real OS error; the advice ledger reports its own write failures. doctor and sync print '(FAILED: err)' per entry and exit 1; validation output still runs after failed repairs so the operator sees the resulting data state (only the exit code gates). The recovery machinery was extracted into domain::release_recovery (T-2) before this landed.

workstreams.name: Doctor trustworthiness
workstreams.summary: Make doctor's release-truth repair honest end to end: see everything on disk, recover imperfect data reversibly with an explicit reconciliation contract, and never claim a repair that did not happen.

evidence.label: PR #259 merged (create-missing-index repair + strict dir-scan filter), verify-rust green
PR #260 merged (union enumeration + E-1 recovery/backup/advice), verify-rust green
PR #261 merged (T-1 fail-loud error channel + T-2 release_recovery extraction), verify-rust green

26 release-truth/recovery/advice/attribution regression tests incl. unix read-only abort, MD5-level backup preservation, suffix-collision attribution, status/apply agreement (cargo test -p architext-core --lib)
